summary: Independent QA review found the M2 task graph broadly covers Roadmap M2, the acceptance matrix, both M1 Gate follow-ups, and stays within planning scope, but two client task dependency edges are missing and can make tasks ready before their required read-model sources exist.

tests_run.result: Modified coordination files present: project/goal-mode-state.json, project/model-routing-state.json, project/tasks/thread-registry.json. Untracked systems handoff, two lead-owned route files, and 12 M2 task files are present. No product code or docs outside the planning artifacts appeared in status.
Listed foundation and SIM tasks as CLOSED, M1-GATE as CLOSED, M2-TASK-GRAPH-001 as REVIEW, and 11 downstream M2 tasks as DRAFT.
No output while M2-TASK-GRAPH-001 remains in REVIEW.

tests_run.command: node -e JSON/DAG validation for all project/tasks/active/M2-*.json
tests_run.result: Parsed and shape-checked 12 M2 task files; DAG acyclic; dependencies known. QA review then found two semantically missing dependency edges.
Tracked diffs are limited to coordination state files: project/goal-mode-state.json, project/model-routing-state.json, project/tasks/thread-registry.json.
Untracked files are the systems handoff, two lead-owned route files, and 12 M2 active task JSON files.

tests_run.command: node -e taskctl validate for every project/tasks/active/M2-*.json
tests_run.result: All 12 M2 task JSON files validated individually.

decisions: Coverage is otherwise adequate: the DAG represents the 30 District/10 Settlement fixture, seasonal regional curves, agriculture/labor/population/grain/cash scaffolding, market/cash-flow basics, route/transport basics, map rendering, district panel, 4,000-row list pressure fixture, bad-map/bad-reference rejection, H-001/H-002 initial pressure validation, M2 exit validation, and the M2 map source policy.
M1-GATE-FOLLOW-UP-002 is represented by M2-BOOT-BOUNDARY-001 before economy and route implementation; M1-GATE-FOLLOW-UP-001 is represented by M2-HOST-HASH-CANARY-001 before M2 exit.
The graph is syntactically valid and acyclic, but semantically incomplete because two client tasks can become ready before all data they promise to display is produced.
The diff stayed within M2 planning artifacts, with only lead-owned route files and pre-existing coordination-state changes outside the M2-TASK-GRAPH-001 allowed scope.
risks: If M2-CLIENT-MAP-001 remains dependent only on M2-ROUTE-TRANSPORT-001, lead automation can start it before M2-ECON-POP-001 even though its acceptance criteria require economy map mode/read-model inspection.
If M2-CLIENT-DISTRICT-LIST-001 remains dependent only on M2-ECON-POP-001, lead automation can start it before M2-ROUTE-TRANSPORT-001 even though its acceptance criteria require route summary fields.
blockers: DAG dependency ordering is not yet sensible for the two client tasks that consume both economy and route read-model outputs.
requested_action: Update M2-CLIENT-MAP-001 dependencies to include both M2-ROUTE-TRANSPORT-001 and M2-ECON-POP-001, and update M2-CLIENT-DISTRICT-LIST-001 dependencies to include both M2-ECON-POP-001 and M2-ROUTE-TRANSPORT-001, unless the corresponding acceptance criteria are narrowed to remove economy mode and route summary requirements. Then rerun the required M2-TASK-GRAPH-001 validation commands and emit a revised systems handoff for QA review.
